_comment: Curated 'before picture' for task 0003: positions where the rating-BLIND Lichess Win% baseline mischaracterizes practical chances. `engine_cp` is White-POV centipawns; for the drawn studies it is 0 by endgame theory (an engine reports ~0.00). `hypothesized_practical_white_*` are NOT measurements — they are hypotheses the rating-sliced 0002 data must confirm/refute (see README 'Deferred'). All positions are White-to-move, so engine_cp is already White's POV.

positions.name: Philidor position (R+P vs R)
positions.engine_note: Theoretically drawn with the third-rank defence; engine ~0.00.
positions.why_baseline_misleads: Baseline reads 50/50, but the defence is a known technique most sub-2000 players don't hold — real results skew to the stronger side.

positions.name: Opposite-coloured bishops, Black up a pawn
positions.engine_note: Opposite-coloured bishop endgame a pawn up — a textbook fortress draw; engine ~0.00.
positions.why_baseline_misleads: Baseline says 50/50; in practice the side up a pawn keeps pressing and weaker defenders occasionally crack, so outcomes are not symmetric.

positions.name: K+P vs K, defender holds the opposition
positions.engine_note: Defender has the opposition with White to move — drawn; engine 0.00.
positions.why_baseline_misleads: Baseline reads 50/50, which happens to match the result here — but only if the defender knows the opposition. Beginners lose this; the metric can't see who is playing.
positions.source: Basic K+P vs K opposition theory

positions.name: Wrong-coloured bishop + rook pawn
positions.engine_note: Bishop does not control the h8 promotion square and the defending king reaches the corner — dead draw; engine 0.00 despite White being up B+P.
positions.why_baseline_misleads: An objective bar over material would scream 'winning'; the truth is 0.00. Baseline (over a real engine) gets this right — included to show the 'material illusion' the engine, but not a naive bar, resolves.

positions.name: Saavedra position (win only via underpromotion)
positions.engine_note: White is winning, but ONLY through 6.c8=R!! (queening stalemates). Engine shows a large advantage.
positions.why_baseline_misleads: Baseline maps the engine's '+winning' to ~97% for White, but the win hinges on a rook underpromotion almost no sub-master finds — practically the position is close to a draw at club level.

positions.name: Réti study (draw only via the king diagonal)
positions.engine_note: Drawn, but ONLY via the paradoxical king march Kg7-Kf6-Ke5 that simultaneously chases the h-pawn and supports c6. Engine 0.00.
positions.why_baseline_misleads: Baseline reads 50/50 (correct objectively), but the draw depends on a counterintuitive line weak players miss — White usually loses in practice, so 50% overstates White's real chances at low ratings.

positions.name: Knight-promotion fork (win only via e8=N+)
positions.engine_note: White wins a queen with 1.e8=N+! forking Kg7 and Qf6; 1.e8=Q is not even check and stays roughly equal. Engine shows White ~winning.
positions.why_baseline_misleads: Baseline maps the engine's '+winning' to ~95% for White, but the advantage exists ONLY through an underpromotion fork many sub-1500 players miss (auto-queening throws it away) — practically far closer to equal.
positions.source: Knight-underpromotion fork motif (composed, verifiable by hand)
